author: @VaderPopsVicodin10
text:
Met Tom Cruise once, when we visited New York City. I saw him walking to an elevator as I was getting off the elevator. He saw me eyeball him with recognition, so I put my hand out and just said I loved his work..He actually genuinely smiled, shook my hand, thanked me and asked me my name..I told him, he thanked me again with that crazy Cruise smile and said "Pleasure meeting you, Michael" and got on to the elevator..he had a bodyguard with him, but he was very genuine in his actions. It surprised me. It all lasted just about 3-5 seconds, but 🤷🏻‍♂️..Idk, I thought it was pretty cool that I just met the dude and he couldn't have been nicer.. Always been a Cruise fan.. and still hoping and waiting for an "Edge of Tomorrow" sequel. 
__
Edit: This was mid 2000s, around the release of War of the Worlds.

replies.author: @crazyralph6386
replies.text:
I’ve met him at the Burbank FBO a couple of times while fuelling up to fight fires and he was a cool cat. We chatted about my company A-star helicopter (apparently he owns 2?) cause it had the updated glass instrument panel. 

Never asked for an autograph or even mentioned being a fan, just a couple of aviation enthusiasts shooting the poop. He probably got into flying for that reason, as most pilots aren’t star struck. 

One person you NEVER talk to apparently, is that crusty old fart Harrison Ford. He’s known to be a miserable sob, who everyone avoids like the plague. 😂

replies.author: @Rodesium
replies.text: @Spiritstealer Those were the regulations enforced by the government. If one person on the set didn't comply, the entire production could have been shut down. People would lose their jobs, the monetary loss would be massive. In this context it doesn't matter that particle filters don't stop aerosol (they don't.) He wanted the movie to be filmed, so everybody, including him, had to bend over and obey. That doesn't make him unhinged, just like wearing a mask on the subway when going to work didn't make me unhinged either. I have bills to pay. I can't just not show up, and no, I couldn't work remote, not as a first responder.

replies.author: @Spiritstealer
replies.text: @Rodesium he was still being an ass about it. He wasn’t even polite about it, he was unhinged. He could have simple spoke to the person like an adult.

replies.author: @PhantomFilmAustralia
replies.text: He snapped during shooting "Eyes Wide Shut".  Having Kubrick listen to none of his ideas, making Cruise do 95 takes to just walk through a doorway, and having the shoot blow out to 400 days broke Cruise.  He had never felt so miserable and shut down.  Since then he's wanted a large degree of creative control and stunt performing in his movies.  It's why he's also the producer on nearly every Mission: Impossible movie.  He can do whatever he wants.

author: @Jared_Wignall
text:
“I’ve had conversations with friends of mine and they all ask about why I don’t make a film about the things I care about politically, and I always say, ‘Well, because it doesn’t work.’ You can’t use narrative to tell people what to think. It never works. People just react against it. You have to be purer about it. You have to be truer to the principles of narrative and telling a story, and that means risking misinterpretation. 
 It doesn’t mean you don’t care about something, or that it doesn’t mean anything to you, but you have to be neutral or objective in your approach. You can’t tell people what to think, you can only invite them to feel something. I always think of the great moment in Gladiator, where he chops off his head and then turns to the audience and asks them, ‘Are you not entertained?’ Whatever it is, you’ve got to entertain.” -Christopher Nolan.

author: @jackburtonstwin
text: Well played, Tom. The media are not your friends, they are not hanging on your nuanced response, they are seeking a gotcha moment for media rage bait. Tom has been at the top for long enough not to be mugged off by these f'ing half-wits. He is probably the last true movie superstar aware that his brand hangs on his movies not his political hot takes. The privileges his career has afforded him removes him from any real link to actual working people and he's smart enough not to go piss on their legs and tell them its raining.

author: @jrddoubleu514
text:
Tom Cruise is the best. I was a little boy when I first saw him in Mission Impossible, and I watch all his movies, past and present.

I know some people aren't keen on him.

I think he's one of the best actors in the world. 

His response is exactly what I'd expect.

'I dont care about that. I care about this'

'Ask me about this, or go away. I'm not here to indulge your nonsense.'

Basically.

I hope he remains on screen for another 30 years.

If we had the science, he'd give us 10/10 blockbuster action forever.

'I'm here to make movies. To entertain and awe the people even when the world is going to S'
